license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yvhfw36
title: Preparation of human Red Blood Cells for Scanning Electron Microscopy imaging
author: Marie Anne Balanant

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare glass coverslips by incubating them in 10 % volume poly-D-lysine solution in PBS for 00:30:00 at RT
Dry and keep at 4 °C until use
Wash 100 µL of RBC in 900 µL PBS ( 00:02:00, at 1000g )
Resuspend 25 µL of the RBC suspension in 475 µL of PBS

Add 500 µL of 2 % volume glutaraldehyde solution in PBS to the cell suspension slowly (drop by drop) to reach a final glutaraldehyde concentration of 1% Work under a fume hood when using glutaraldehyde
Wash with 1 mL of PBS ( 00:02:00, at 1000g )
Dilute 100 µL of fixed cells suspension into 400 µL PBS
Incubate 200 µL of diluted cell suspension on a poly-D-lysine coated coverslip ( 01:00:00 ) in corresponding buffer (in 6-wells plates)
Rince slide 2 times with PBS (without scratching/damaging cell layer)
Incubate in 0.1 Molarity (M) cacodylate buffer ( 00:10:00 ) . Repeat step 3 times Work under a fume hood during this section, check waste disposal for the different chemicals
Post-fix with 1 Mass Percent osmium tetroxide ( 01:00:00 )

Dehydrate the sample with ethanol - Ethanol 40 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 50 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 60 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 70 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 80 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 90 % volume ( 00:10:00 ) - Ethanol 100 % volume ( 00:15:00 ) - Ethanol 100 % volume ( 00:15:00 )
Incubate in HDMS ( 00:30:00 ). Repear step twice
Dry under the hood for 00:30:00 minimum (place filter paper under coverslip to prevent attaching to the culture plate)
Cover with a layer of gold palladium
